title: Question: How to pack arrays properly in my C++ test bench?
body:
--- Author Name: **David Banas** (@capn-freako) Original Redmine Message: 2827 from https://www.veripool.org --- I'm confused about how to properly pack arrays in my C++ test bench, because the port types in my resultant @V.h@ file appear to be schizophrenic. For instance, take this example: ``` VL_INW(weights,607,0,19); ``` If I trace through the chain of included *.h files, I find the macro above resolves to: ``` unsigned long weights[19]; ``` giving me: 64 * 19 = 1,216 bits. But, the port only requires 608 bits, as seen above. So, what's the proper procedure for packing this array with data for use by the Verilated model?
Do I just ignore the upper 32 bits of every long?
